title: tragacantha monadelpha
intro: Tragacantha monadelpha, also known as the Bristle Bush or Bristle Senna, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is a thorny shrub native to the Mediterranean region, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and stimulate bowel movements. Tragacantha monadelpha has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including constipation, digestive problems, and skin conditions, due to its anthraquinone glycosides content. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by steeping its leaves and flowers in hot water, allowing for a concentrated dose of its medicinal compounds.
